_comment: End-to-end filesystem test. readwritePaths are exposed to the guest under /host/<basename>. Before running, create /tmp/hyperlight-fs-demo/ and drop a file in it (or let the script create one). After the run, /tmp/hyperlight-fs-demo/written.txt will exist with guest-written content.

process.commandLine:
import os

BASE = '/host/hyperlight-fs-demo'
os.makedirs(BASE, exist_ok=True)

# Write
path = f'{BASE}/hello.txt'
with open(path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('hello, filesystem!\n')
    f.write('line 2\n')
print(f'wrote: {path}')

# Read back
with open(path, 'r') as f:
    print(f'read:\n{f.read()}')

# Append
with open(path, 'a') as f:
    f.write('appended line\n')

# Stat
st = os.stat(path)
print(f'size: {st.st_size} bytes')

# Listdir
print(f'files in {BASE}: {sorted(os.listdir(BASE))}')

# Write a marker so the host can verify
with open(f'{BASE}/written.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('hyperlight was here\n')
print('done')
